verdict: PARTIALLY INCONSISTENT. Multiple authoritative surfaces exist; my new terminology overlaps but is NOT aligned with the canonical runtime vocabulary.
summary: There are at least four overlapping agent-definition surfaces in TNF. My work addressed the DB-vendor side but missed the runtime 'traits' vocabulary that `tnf register`, `tnf traits list`, `tnf agents bank reconcile`, and `DACC-v1 ROLE_DEFINITIONS.md` already speak.

vocabulary_surfaces_found.my_work_alignment: MISSED. None of {role, fulfillment, qualities} map onto DACC roles.

vocabulary_surfaces_found.scope: runtime CLI default arguments, `tnf traits list` output, `tnf register` defaults
vocabulary_surfaces_found.my_work_alignment: PARTIAL. I added 4 new enum values CLI_KILO/CLI_OPENCODE/CLI_PI/API_CLAUDE_CODE inside the legacy 110-value agentTypeEnum — these do NOT coincide with the canonical AGENT_PLATFORM_TRAITS list, and 'kilo'/'opencode' still cannot be passed to `tnf register <platform>`.
OK for kilo+opencode (matches); MISSED for 'project' as a bank target vs my enum mapping.
This is the surface I tried to fix with role+fulfillment split. PPE: my AgentRole enum (60 values) collides with both DACC and AGENT_ROLE_TRAITS.

vocabulary_surfaces_found.scope: DB column + in-memory + broker dispatch tie-breaker
vocabulary_surfaces_found.my_work_alignment: INTERNAL CONSISTENT, but conflicts with established vocabularies.

inconsistencies_found.title: `role` is overloaded across four vocabularies
details: My AgentRole enum (0006): code_generation / orchestrator / cli_coder / etc. (worker-action primitives)
inconsistencies_found.impact: An agent registered with role='orchestrator' (mine) is technically compatible with role='orchestrator' (DACC and traits) but means DIFFERENT things. With DACC, 'orchestrator' is the master-clock baton holder. With my schema, 'orchestrator' is just a worker that coordinates things. The agent-bank/broker layers see only one of these.
inconsistencies_found.remediation: Add a new column `dacc_role` enum with ('director','orchestrator','broker','worker','participant'). Keep my role enum as `worker_action` or rename to `action_primitive`. Make DACC the canonical identity axis; make my enum the action-this-worker-performs axis.

inconsistencies_found.title: `platform` is split across two vocabularies
details: AGENT_PLATFORM_TRAITS (cli.ts:2959): antigravity, gemini, claude, jules, pi, vscode, browser (7 values)
Bank targets (cli.ts:9818): codex, claude, gemini, opencode, kilo, augment, tnf, hermes, project (9 values)
My agentTypeEnum additions: CLI_KILO, CLI_OPENCODE, CLI_PI, API_CLAUDE_CODE
MISSING from AGENT_PLATFORM_TRAITS: kilo, opencode, hermes (works against canonical `tnf register <platform>`)
MISSING from agent-bank targets: pi, vscode, browser
inconsistencies_found.impact: If a user runs `tnf register myagent worker kilo`, the CLI rejects or silently ignores 'kilo' because it isn't in AGENT_PLATFORM_TRAITS. My fix added enum values for DB persistence but did not expose kilo/opencode through `tnf register`.
inconsistencies_found.remediation: Update AGENT_PLATFORM_TRAITS to include 'kilo','opencode','hermes','codex','augment'. Sync with the bank target map (probably the bank-target list should BE the merged platform taxonomy).

inconsistencies_found.title: `traits` is a canonical term I silently replaced
inconsistencies_found.details: TNF already uses 'traits' (tnf traits list, AGENT_ROLE_TRAITS, AGENT_PLATFORM_TRAITS, REDIS_COMMAND_TRAITS, PROVIDER_ROUTED_COMMAND_TRAITS). My new field is called 'qualities' — different name, overlapping concept.
inconsistencies_found.remediation: Rename my `qualities` field -> `traits`. Keep the type structure but expose via `tnf agents traits list`.

inconsistencies_found.title: manifestations of 'metadata' exist but I did not mine them
details: agents table: column `metadata` (jsonb, exists pre-audit) and `config` (jsonb)
TNF reserved AGENT_METADATA, HTTP_AGENT_REGISTRY_KEY, broker metadata-flow
inconsistencies_found.impact: I added `fulfillment` and `qualities` as new jsonb columns when the codebase already has 'metadata' as a reserved-but-unstructured bag. Several places (e.g. master_agent_registry.ts MasterAgentProfile.metadata) already use 'metadata' as the catch-all for personality/version/communicationStyle/etc.
inconsistencies_found.remediation: Decide policy: are `fulfillment` and `qualities` top-level columns (good for indexing) or should they nest inside `agents.metadata`? Both choices have merit; the choice must be explicit and paired with the existing `agents_metadata` table semantics.

inconsistencies_found.title: Promoted but not in LIVING_STATE / Active Agents
inconsistencies_found.details: My Phase 4 seeder inserts kilo-cli, opencode-cli, pi-coding-agent, claude-code, tnf-hermes, jules as TB rows in the registry with the SYSTEM user. The AGENT_STATUS_LEDGER.md, however, has canonical named agents like Antigravity, Historian/Journalist, Forge Agent, Codex, Orchestrator. My new entries are stored in DB but not 'visible' to the existing ledger view that operators actually read.
inconsistencies_found.remediation: When seeding, also append to AGENT_STATUS_LEDGER.md as a standing-by entry. Or — better — make `tnf traits list agent_roles` derive from the registry so the two views cannot drift.

inconsistencies_found.title: Fulfillment is a UNIQUE new concept — risk of single-source-of-truth leak
inconsistencies_found.details: Fulfillment{model,transport,protocol_version,prompt_doc_uri,tools,endpoint,vendor}  is a real, useful concept — but I have not yet ratified it as the canonical term. Other places (multi-agent-integration protocol docs, twip, and possibly further registry work) may use the same idea under different names: 'runtime', 'engine', 'binder', 'driver', 'execution-stack'.
inconsistencies_found.remediation: Search docs/protocols/MULTI_AGENT_INTEGRATION_PROTOCOL.md and TWIP docs for existing terminology; explicitly state 'fulfillment' as the canonical term in the audit doc and link to it from ROLE_DEFINITIONS.md.

summary_user_facing: No, there is not full consistency. The Phase 1-7 work added DB-schema rigor and made the broker fulfillment-aware, but the resulting taxonomy overlaps and partially conflicts with the canonical runtime vocabulary in `tnf traits` and DACC-v1 ROLE_DEFINITIONS. Specifically: 'role' is overloaded (4 meanings), 'platform' lives in 2 different vocabularies, the term 'traits' should replace my 'qualities', and the ledger / trait-list views cannot see my new entries.

do_now_options.action: Run Phase 8: a consistency-alignment pass that (a) renames qualities->traits, (b) adds explicit dacc_role enum (director/orchestrator/broker/worker/participant), keeps my agent_role enum as a separate field called worker_action, (c) compiles a single merged PLATFORM vocabulary from AGENT_PLATFORM_TRAITS + bank-targets, and (d) wires `tnf traits list` to derive from the registry instead of being a hard-coded JS array. About 4-6 hours of work.
Document the divergence in a compatibility note (already partially done in this file) and accept that there are two vocabularies until a future cleanup. Lower cost, higher ongoing technical debt.

Revert Phase 1-7 and replace with a single Phase-8 that ONLY edits the runtime trait arrays + tweaks the in-memory registry — never touching the DB schema. Smaller blast radius, loses DB-tier classification work.

key_files_referenced_in_this_review: packages/tnf-cli/src/cli.ts:2958 (AGENT_ROLE_TRAITS), 2959 (AGENT_PLATFORM_TRAITS), 4596 (tnf register defaults), 9618 (tnf traits cmd), 9808-9850 (tnf agents bank reconcile)
next_move_recommended: Option A. TNF protocol mandates non-temporal proliferation; vocabulary drift is exactly the kind of thing that should be unified at the source. Run a Phase 8 with the changes above before announcing 'consistency achieved'.
